---
title: "Week 4 Assessment: String Methods"
subtitle: "Applying String Transformation and Search Methods"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: false
  pdf:
    toc: true
---

# String Methods Assessment

**Name:** _________________________ **Date:** _____________

**Instructions:** Choose the appropriate string method(s) to solve each problem. Some problems require method chaining.

---

## Part 1: Method Selection (30 points)

For each task, write the method name that accomplishes it:

1. Convert "hello" to "HELLO" → `._____()` 

2. Remove spaces from "  text  " → `._____()` 

3. Find position of "@" in email → `._____("@")` 

4. Replace "old" with "new" → `._____("old", "new")` 

5. Check if string contains only letters → `._____()` 

6. Split "a,b,c" into list → `._____(",")` 

7. Convert "HELLO" to "Hello" → `._____()` 

8. Count occurrences of "a" → `._____("a")` 

9. Check if string starts with "http" → `._____("http")` 

10. Join list ["a", "b", "c"] with "-" → `"-"._____()` 

---

## Part 2: Method Application (30 points)

Write the code to accomplish each task:

### Problem 1: Name Standardization
```python
name = "  jOHn  SMitH  "

# Clean and format to "John Smith"
clean_name = name._____()._____()
# Need to handle internal spaces too
final_name = ________________________________
```

### Problem 2: Email Validation
```python
email = "  USER@DOMAIN.COM  "

# Clean and lowercase
clean_email = email._____()._____()

# Check if valid format (has @ and .)
has_at = _____ in clean_email
has_dot = _____ in clean_email
at_pos = clean_email._____("@")
dot_after_at = "." in clean_email[_____:]

valid = has_at and has_dot and dot_after_at
```

### Problem 3: Password Checking
```python
password = "Pass123"

# Check requirements
long_enough = len(password) >= _____
has_upper = any(c._____ for c in password)
has_lower = any(c._____ for c in password)
has_digit = any(c._____ for c in password)

strong = long_enough and has_upper and has_lower and has_digit
```

---

## Part 3: Method Chaining (20 points)

Complete these method chains:

1. Clean and uppercase:
```python
text = "  hello world  \n"
result = text._____()._____()  # "HELLO WORLD"
```

2. Extract and clean domain:
```python
email = "  John.Doe@Example.COM  "
# Get just "example.com"
domain = email._____()._____(___)._____(___)[___]._____()
```

3. Format title:
```python
title = "the lord of the rings"
# Want: "The Lord Of The Rings"
formatted = title._____()
```

4. Remove all spaces:
```python
text = "h e l l o  w o r l d"
# Want: "helloworld"
no_spaces = text._____(" ", "")
```

---

## Part 4: Real-World Problems (30 points)

### Problem 1: Parse CSV Line (10 points)
```python
line = "John,Doe,25,Engineer"

# Split into parts
parts = line._____(___)

# Extract and clean each field
first_name = parts[0]._____()
last_name = parts[1]._____()
age = int(parts[2])
job = parts[3]._____()

# Create formatted output
output = f"{first_name} {last_name} ({age}) - {job}"
```

### Problem 2: Clean Phone Number (10 points)
```python
phone = "(555) 123-4567"

# Extract just digits
digits = ""
for char in phone:
    if char._____():
        digits = digits + char

# Format as xxx-xxx-xxxx
if len(digits) == 10:
    formatted = digits[___:___] + "-" + digits[___:___] + "-" + digits[___:___]
```

### Problem 3: Word Analysis (10 points)
```python
text = """Python is great.
Python is easy to learn.
I love Python."""

# Count sentences (periods)
sentence_count = text._____(".")

# Count word "Python" (case-insensitive)
python_count = text._____()._____("python")

# Get all words
words = text._____(" ", " ")._____("\n", " ")._____()

# Find longest word
longest = ""
for word in words:
    # Remove punctuation from word
    clean_word = word._____(".", "")._____(",", "")
    if len(clean_word) > len(longest):
        longest = clean_word
```

---

## Part 5: Error Detection (10 points)

Find and fix the errors in this code:

```python
# Problem 1
name = "john doe"
name.title()  # Doesn't change name!
print(name)   # Still lowercase

# Fix:
name = _________________

# Problem 2
email = "User@Domain.Com"
if email.lower == "user@domain.com":  # Won't work!
    print("Match")

# Fix:
if email._____ == "user@domain.com":
    print("Match")

# Problem 3
text = "  hello  "
clean = text.strip(" hello")  # Not what you think!

# Fix (to remove spaces):
clean = text._____()

# Problem 4
words = "one,two,three"
list = words.split()  # Wrong delimiter!

# Fix:
list = words._____(___)
```

---

## Part 6: Challenge Problem (10 points bonus)

Create a function-like behavior without using `def`:

```python
# Clean any messy input
messy = "  hELLo   WoRLD!!! \n\t"

# Your solution: clean, normalize case, remove extra punctuation
# Should result in: "Hello World!"

# Step 1: Strip whitespace
step1 = messy._____()

# Step 2: Proper case
step2 = step1._____()

# Step 3: Remove extra punctuation (tricky!)
# Hint: Use replace multiple times
step3 = step2
while "!!" in step3:
    step3 = step3._____("!!", "!")
    
# Final result
clean = step3
```

---

## Grading Rubric

| Section | Points | Your Score |
|---------|---------|------------|
| Part 1: Method Selection | 30 |   |
| Part 2: Method Application | 30 |   |
| Part 3: Method Chaining | 20 |   |
| Part 4: Real-World | 30 |   |
| Part 5: Error Detection | 10 |   |
| Part 6: Bonus | 10 |   |
| **Total** | **120** (+10) |   |

**Grade:** _______

---

## Method Reference Card

| Method | Description | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `.upper()` | Convert to uppercase | `"hi".upper()` → "HI" |
| `.lower()` | Convert to lowercase | `"HI".lower()` → "hi" |
| `.title()` | Title case | `"hello world".title()` → "Hello World" |
| `.strip()` | Remove edge spaces | `" hi ".strip()` → "hi" |
| `.find()` | Find substring position | `"hello".find("l")` → 2 |
| `.replace()` | Replace text | `"hi".replace("i", "o")` → "ho" |
| `.split()` | Split into list | `"a,b".split(",")` → ["a", "b"] |
| `.join()` | Join list items | `"-".join(["a", "b"])` → "a-b" |
| `.count()` | Count occurrences | `"hello".count("l")` → 2 |
| `.isalpha()` | Check if letters only | `"abc".isalpha()` → True |
| `.isdigit()` | Check if digits only | `"123".isdigit()` → True |
| `.startswith()` | Check start | `"hi".startswith("h")` → True |

---

## Answer Key (For Instructor Use)

### Part 1
1. `.upper()`
2. `.strip()`
3. `.find("@")`
4. `.replace("old", "new")`
5. `.isalpha()`
6. `.split(",")`
7. `.title()` or `.capitalize()`
8. `.count("a")`
9. `.startswith("http")`
10. `"-".join()`

[Complete solutions provided separately]
